task: Lock final scope & feature list for MVP (write it down, freeze it)
Domain purchased + DNS configured
Hosting set up (Vercel/Netlify/Cloudflare) + SSL
Core UI built (landing + key flows)
PWA manifest.json configured (name, icons, theme, display:standalone)
Service worker for offline caching + install prompt
App icons exported (192, 512, maskable, favicon, apple-touch)
Responsive on mobile / tablet / desktop
Forms working + backend wired (sign-up, contact, waitlist)
Error states, empty states, loading states
SEO meta tags, OG image, sitemap.xml, robots.txt
Privacy policy (cookies, GA, third-party services disclosed)
Terms of service
Cookie consent banner (GDPR if EU traffic)
Copy: hero, value prop, features, FAQ, footer
Brand assets ready (logo SVG/PNG, color tokens, fonts)
Every CTA, link, and form submission tested
Cross-browser: Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge
Mobile: iOS Safari + Android Chrome (real devices)
PWA install flow tested on iOS + Android
Offline mode behaves correctly
Lighthouse audit — aim 90+ on PWA, Performance, A11y, SEO
Image optimization (WebP, lazy load, correct sizes)
Accessibility: keyboard nav, alt text, ARIA, color contrast
404 page + error boundaries
Load test backend (whatever you expect on day 1, x5)
Google Analytics 4 property created + GTM container
GA snippet installed, verified in real-time view
Conversion events defined (sign-up, install, key click)
Meta Pixel / LinkedIn Insight Tag (if running paid)
Microsoft Clarity or Hotjar for session recording
UTM convention documented for all campaigns
Create LinkedIn company page (logo, banner, tagline, about)
Add founders/team as page admins, link personal profiles
Update founder personal profiles with company info
Plan 5–7 launch-week posts (teaser, story, problem, demo, live)
Engage in 3–5 relevant communities/groups for 1 week pre-launch
Pick 1–2 more channels (X/Twitter, Instagram, TikTok) based on audience
Handles secured, profiles set up consistently
Launch content calendar (pre-launch 1 week + launch week)
Pick provider (Substack / Beehiiv / Mailchimp / ConvertKit)
Welcome email + double opt-in configured
Embed signup on website + dedicated landing page
Draft issue #1 — launch announcement
Cadence decided (weekly? bi-weekly?) + topic list for first 4 issues
Personal network list: 50–100 people to message individually on launch day
Draft DM/email templates (personalized variables)
Press list: 5–10 journalists/newsletters in your space
Plan Product Hunt launch (if relevant) — needs prep 1–2 weeks ahead
Final smoke test in production at 8am
Publish announcement on LinkedIn (company + personal)
Send newsletter issue #1
Send personal DMs/emails to launch list (batch through the day)
Post in relevant communities (Slack/Discord/subreddits)
Reply to every comment / DM / signup within 1 hour
Monitor GA, error logs, performance every 2 hours
Weekly metrics review (signups, retention, drop-off points)
User interviews — 5–10 in first 2 weeks
Bug triage + hotfix cadence
Identify top 3 friction points from data + interviews
Roadmap for native app reflecting MVP learnings
Newsletter issue #2, #3, #4 — establish rhythm
LinkedIn: 3 posts/week consistently
First piece of long-form content (blog or LinkedIn article)
Set up referral / waitlist incentive if applicable
Pick stack (React Native / Flutter / native iOS+Android / Capacitor)
Apple Developer account ($99/yr) + Google Play account ($25 one-time)
Port MVP features + add app-only capabilities (push, deep links, etc.)
Internal alpha — TestFlight (iOS) + Internal Testing track (Android)
Closed beta with 20–50 users from MVP waitlist
App icons + splash screens at all required sizes
Screenshots for all device sizes (6.7", 6.5", 5.5", iPad, Android)
App Store listing: name, subtitle, description, keywords
Privacy nutrition labels (Apple) + data safety form (Google)
Demo account for app review
Submit to App Store — allow 2–7 days review
Submit to Play Store — allow up to 7 days for first review
Coordinated launch — newsletter, LinkedIn, personal outreach again
Update website to "Download on App Store / Get it on Google Play"
PWA → native app upgrade prompt for existing users
Press / Product Hunt round 2
Company incorporated + founder agreement signed
Business bank account opened
Accounting tool set up (Xero / QuickBooks / Zoho)
IP: trademark name + logo, decide on patents if relevant
Standard contracts: NDA, employment, contractor, customer ToS
Workspace tools: Google Workspace, Slack, Notion/Linear
Password manager (1Password / Bitwarden) for shared credentials
Weekly cadence: standup, metrics review, retro
Hiring plan for next 2 roles (if applicable)
Cap table cleaned up
Pitch deck v1 (problem, solution, market, traction, ask)
Investor list — 30–50 relevant angels / funds
Financial model (12–18mo runway view)
Data room with metrics, deck, cap table, key docs
Support inbox + help@ email routing
Help center / FAQ docs
Status page (statuspage.io / instatus / self-hosted)
NPS or CSAT collection at 2-week mark
Create Supabase project + get API keys
Set up Google OAuth in Google Cloud Console + connect to Supabase
Install Supabase SDK in React app + configure .env
Build login screen with 'Continue with Google' button (branded)
Wire up auth state check + protected routes
Build logout + 'who am I' user menu
Add email/password as fallback login option
Test full login flow on mobile (iOS Safari + Android Chrome)
Open a doc. Write down all 6 Yuki features: Home, Pip Chat, Daily Routine, Office Planner, Journal, Rewards
For EACH feature, write 1 sentence: 'In MVP, this lets users [X]'. Keep it simple.
List anything you're tempted to add but will NOT include in MVP. Add to 'v1.1 later' list.
Share the doc with co-founder/advisor for sanity check
Mark scope as 'FROZEN' in a visible place. No more additions until after launch.
Go to namecheap.com or cloudflare.com/products/registrar
Check availability for: yuki.app, getyuki.com, meetyuki.com, useyuki.com, hellyuki.com
Buy the .app or .com that's available (₹800-1500/yr)
Enable WHOIS privacy (free or ~$1) so your address isn't public
Note login credentials in password manager
Create a Vercel account at vercel.com (free with GitHub login)
Connect your Yuki GitHub repo to Vercel
Trigger a first deployment — should auto-deploy on git push
Add your custom domain in Vercel dashboard → point DNS to Vercel
Verify the live site loads on https://yourdomain.com
List all screens needed for MVP: Login, Home, Pip Chat, Routine, Planner, Journal, Rewards, Settings
Build static layouts for each (no logic yet) — just fake data and styling
Add navigation between screens (bottom tab bar for mobile)
Style with Yuki tokens: Fraunces + Poppins, pink/black palette, pastel cards
Test the full click-through journey: Login → Home → each feature → back
Create /public/manifest.json in your project
Set name='Yuki', short_name='Yuki', theme_color='#E91E63'
Set display='standalone' so it opens like a native app
Set start_url='/' and scope='/'
Add icons array with 192x192 and 512x512 PNGs
Link the manifest in index.html <head> with <link rel='manifest'>
Test in Chrome DevTools → Application → Manifest tab to verify no errors
Install Workbox or use Vite PWA plugin: 'npm install vite-plugin-pwa'
Configure plugin in vite.config.js with basic offline caching
Test offline mode: open site → kill wifi → reload → should still work
Add 'Add to Home Screen' prompt logic (listen for beforeinstallprompt event)
Show a custom 'Install Yuki' button when prompt is available
In Figma or Photoshop: design Pip-based icon at 1024x1024 (master)
Export 192x192 PNG
Export 512x512 PNG
Export 512x512 'maskable' version (Pip with 80% safe zone padding)
Export favicon.ico (32x32)
Export apple-touch-icon.png (180x180) for iOS
Place all in /public folder + reference in manifest.json
Open Yuki in Chrome DevTools → device toolbar → test iPhone SE (375px)
Fix any horizontal scrolling or cramped layouts
Test on iPad (768px) — make sure nothing looks broken
Test on desktop (1280px+) — set max-width so it doesn't look stretched
Get a real phone and load the site — DevTools lies sometimes
Decide: signup goes through Auth (Google) — see Auth subtasks
Build journal entry form → save to Supabase 'journals' table
Build task creation form → save to Supabase 'tasks' table
Build mood/emotion check-in form → save to 'emotions' table
Add basic error handling: show toast on failure, retry button
Test each form end-to-end: submit → data appears in Supabase dashboard
Go to termly.io or iubenda.com → start free privacy policy generator
Answer questions: company name, what data you collect, third parties
List third parties: Supabase, Google (auth), Google Analytics, Microsoft Clarity
Generate the policy → copy HTML or link
Add to your site at /privacy route + link in footer
Bonus: mention you don't sell journal/mental-health data — important for Yuki's trust
Use same generator (termly.io) for Terms of Service
Include sections on: acceptable use, no medical advice disclaimer (CRUCIAL for Yuki), liability limits
Add explicit clause: 'Yuki is not a substitute for professional mental health care'
Publish at /terms + link in footer
Write 3 candidate taglines for the hero. Pick one.
Write the subheadline (≤20 words explaining what Yuki does)
Write the 'features' section: 6 cards, one per feature, with 1-line benefit each
Write FAQ section: 5 questions (price, privacy, who is this for, what makes it different, when's the app)
Write footer copy: company name, year, links to privacy/terms
Read everything out loud — does it sound human?
notes: If it sounds like a robot, rewrite

task: Open Yuki on phone → click every button → does it do what you expect?
Test signup form: try invalid email, empty fields, valid submission
Test journal entry submission → does it save?
Test mood check-in → does Pip respond appropriately?
Test logout → login → does data persist?
Note every bug in a doc → fix later, don't fix mid-test
Open in Chrome → walk through full flow
Open in Safari (Mac) → repeat
Open in Firefox → repeat
Open in Edge → repeat
notes: Same engine as Chrome but still test

task: Note browser-specific issues (Safari often has CSS quirks)
Get real iPhone → open Safari → load yuki.app
Test PWA install flow on iOS Safari (share → Add to Home Screen)
Get real Android phone → repeat with Chrome
Verify install prompt actually appears on Android
Open installed PWA → does it open in standalone (no browser chrome)?
Open Yuki in Chrome → DevTools (F12) → Lighthouse tab
Run audit on Mobile setting, all categories
Read results — focus on top 3 lowest-scoring issues
Fix Performance issues: optimize images, lazy load, defer JS
Fix Accessibility issues: alt text, contrast, semantic HTML
Re-run audit → aim for 90+ on all four categories
Go to analytics.google.com → sign in with Google
Create new property: 'Yuki Production', timezone, currency
Set up Web data stream with your domain URL
Copy Measurement ID (looks like G-XXXXXXXXXX)
Go to tagmanager.google.com → create container 'Yuki'
Copy GTM snippet → paste into <head> of your site
In GTM: add GA4 Configuration tag with your Measurement ID
Publish GTM container
Make list of events: sign_up_completed, journal_entry_created, task_completed, mood_logged, reward_redeemed, pip_chat_message_sent
In your React code, call gtag('event', 'event_name', {...}) at each moment
In GA4: Admin → Events → mark conversions: sign_up, journal_entry, mood_logged
Test: do each action → verify it appears in GA4 Realtime view
Document your event names in a doc — so you stay consistent later
Go to linkedin.com/company/setup/new
Name: Yuki, Page URL: linkedin.com/company/yukiapp
Upload logo (400x400 PNG with Pip)
Upload banner (1128x191 with tagline + Pip)
Write tagline (≤120 chars): 'Your gentle companion for daily life'
Fill About: problem, solution, who it's for, what's next
Industry: Software Development. Specialty: Mental Wellness Apps
Verify page is live and discoverable
Open Notion or doc → outline 8 posts
Post 1: Origin story — write full draft
Post 2: Problem post — write full draft
Post 3: Behind-the-scenes (designing Pip)
Post 4: Hot take on productivity apps
Post 5: Waitlist ask
Post 6: Demo teaser (record short Loom)

Post 8: Launch day announcement

Pick one (recommend Beehiiv for growth features)
Sign up + create 'Yuki' publication
Set custom subdomain or domain
Upload logo + set brand colors
Write About page (1 paragraph: what subscribers get)
Open Beehiiv/Substack editor → new post
Subject line: 'Yuki is live — your gentle companion is here'
Opening: personal greeting from founder
What Yuki is (2 sentences)
Why you built it (1 short paragraph — founder story)
What to do now: try it, share, reply with feedback
Sign off with your name + Pip illustration
Schedule for 9am 30 May
notes: Don't send manually on launch day

task: Open Outreach List sheet in this tracker
Brain dump 30 names you know personally without overthinking
Now go through LinkedIn connections — add 20 more
WhatsApp/iMessage contacts — add 20 more
For each: fill in channel, tier (1-3), 1-line personal hook
Aim for 60-100 total names
Draft Template A (for Tier 1, close friends): warm, casual
Draft Template B (for Tier 2, acquaintances): friendly but brief
Draft Template C (for press/journalists): professional, specific
Include placeholders: [name], [personal_hook], [why_they_care]
Test by personalizing 3 messages — does it feel natural?
Open yuki.app in incognito → verify it loads
Sign up with a test Google account → verify works end-to-end
Check Supabase dashboard → confirm new user appears
Check GA4 Realtime → confirm pageview fires
Submit a test journal entry → verify it saves
Trigger an error intentionally → verify it doesn't crash app
Check error monitoring (Sentry?) for any pending issues
If anything fails: FIX before announcing. Better delayed than broken.
Open LinkedIn → personal profile
Paste launch post draft from Content Calendar
Attach a hero image of Yuki (or short video of Pip)
Hit Post → IMMEDIATELY copy URL
Switch to Yuki company page → repost there
Drop link in first comment of personal post
Pin the post on your profile
Open Outreach List sheet → filter to Tier 1
For each Tier 1 contact, copy Template A + personalize hook
Send via the channel listed (WhatsApp, LinkedIn DM, email)
Update status to 'Sent' as you go
Move to Tier 2 at 12pm (Template B)
Move to Tier 3 at 4pm (Template B variant)
Go to supabase.com → sign up with GitHub or email
Click 'New Project' → name it 'yuki-mvp'
Pick region closest to your users (Mumbai for India)
Set strong database password → SAVE in password manager
Wait ~2 minutes for project to provision
Copy Project URL and anon key from Settings → API → save to a notes doc
Go to console.cloud.google.com → create new project 'Yuki'
In sidebar search: 'OAuth consent screen' → set up External
Fill: app name 'Yuki', user support email, developer contact
Skip 'Scopes' for now → Save
Add yourself as a Test User
notes: So you can test before publishing

task: Go to 'Credentials' → 'Create Credentials' → 'OAuth 2.0 Client ID'
Application type: Web application, name: 'Yuki Web Client'
Get redirect URI from Supabase: Authentication → Providers → Google → copy callback URL
Paste into Google 'Authorized redirect URIs' → Create
Copy Client ID + Client Secret from Google
In Supabase: Authentication → Providers → Google → toggle ON → paste credentials → Save
In your React project terminal: 'npm install @supabase/supabase-js'
Create file src/lib/supabase.js with createClient setup
Create .env file at project root (if not exists)
Add VITE_SUPABASE_URL=... and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=...
Add .env to .gitignore (CRITICAL — never commit secrets)
Reference env vars in supabase.js using import.meta.env
Restart dev server to pick up env vars
Create components/LoginPage.jsx
Design: centered logo (Pip), tagline, single 'Continue with Google' button
Style with Yuki brand: Fraunces heading, Poppins body, pink accent button
Add Google icon to button (use lucide-react or @google/material-icons)
Wire button to call supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google' })
Add subtle loading state ('Opening Google...') while OAuth happens
Test: clicking button opens Google login → returns to app
Create AuthContext using React Context API
Inside, useEffect: supabase.auth.getSession() to check on mount
Subscribe to auth state changes: supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange
Wrap your App with <AuthProvider>
Create useAuth() hook that returns { user, loading }
In your router: if !user, redirect to /login. If user, allow through.
Show loading spinner while auth state is initializing
Test: incognito window → redirected to login. Logged in → home loads.
Add user menu (avatar in top-right) that opens dropdown
Show user name + email in dropdown header
Add 'Sign out' button at bottom of dropdown
Wire button to supabase.auth.signOut() → redirect to /login
Test: click sign out → redirected → can log back in
Add 'Sign up with email' link below Google button on login page
Build separate /signup page with email + password fields
Wire to supabase.auth.signUp({ email, password })
Build /forgot-password page with email field
Wire to supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail()
Customize Supabase email templates: branded subject lines, Pip in header
Test full flow: signup → check email → confirm → login
Test Google login on iPhone Safari (real device, not simulator)
Test Google login on Android Chrome
Verify: after login, PWA install prompt still works
Verify: after closing PWA and reopening, user is still logged in
Test on iOS in 'standalone' PWA mode (after Add to Home Screen)
Fix any safe-area / notch issues on login screen
